description:
To Read:  

  • Read DYL CH 3: Wayfinding (engagement and energy)
  • Read Quantum Success, Chapter 2:  Upending the Superstition of Materialism

To Do:

  • Create an artifact that represents your North Star.
    • You should build this (don't just grab something from your junk drawer).
    • Some ideas:
      • Write a poem
      • Write a song
      • Draw or paint or AI a picture
      • Build a sculpture
      • Write a story or essay
    • Bring the artifact to class and be prepared to present your artifact to your classmates and discuss why and how it represents your North Star

To Submit:

  • North Star Artifact (document, photo, or recording)
name: Session 3: North Star Refinement

description:
To Read:

  • Read: The Road to Character Introduction
  • Watch: Resume Virtues vs Eulogy Virtues

To Do:

  • You have lived the life you’ve been designing, complete with your values, your North Star, and things that engage you and give you energy.  Now, after a full life, people are gathered for your funeral to honor you.  Write the eulogy that gets delivered on this occasion.

To Submit:

  • Written Eulogy
name: Session 4: A Life Well Lived (Eulogy)

description:

    • Week 1
    • Daily entries

To Submit:

name: Session 5: Clifton Strengths

description:
To Read:

  • Read: Tribe, Brand, Domain (TBD)
  • Watch: How Great Leaders Inspire

To Do:

  • Tribe Activity
    • Identify the people with whom you would be most excited to partner/team up with in the following scenarios.  Choose 5 people for each scenario.  Make sure to think about complementary skills to get the job done.  Also, explain why you chose each person to be on your team for each scenario.  
      • If you could hand pick your study group, who would be in it?
      • Who would you choose to travel the world with?
      • Who would you start a business with?
      • What co-workers did you hangout with after work?
      • If you could create your own district as a missionary, who would be in that district?
      • If you were to build your own Amazing Race team who would be on it?
  • Continue Good Time Journal (Week 2)

To Submit:

  • Tribe Activity
name: Session 6: Your Tribe

description:
To Read:  

  • DYL CH 4 - Getting Unstuck

To Do:

  • Mind Maps & Odyssey Stories
    • Taking previous work done around Brand Mind Mapping, Critical Path Exercise, Domain, and Tribe, create three alternative Odyssey Stories
    • Alternative Ideas:
      • Life One - centered on what you’ve already got in mind
      • Life Two - That thing you’d do if thing one were suddenly gone
      • Life Three - If you knew you could make a decent living at it and you knew no one would laugh at you or think less of you for doing it, what would you do?
    • Give each idea a 6 word title and write down three questions that arise out of each version of you
  • Continue Good Time Journal (Week 3) 

To Submit:

  • 3 Alternate 5-year plans, each to include:
    • A visual/graphical timeline. Include personal and non career events as well—do you want to be married, train to win the CrossFit Games, or learn how to bend spoons with your mind?
    • A title for each option in the form of a six-word headline describing the essence of this alternative.
    • Questions that this alternative is asking—preferably two or three. A good designer asks questions to test assumptions and reveal new insights. In each potential timeline, you will investigate different possibilities and learn different things about yourself and the world. What kinds of things will you want to test and explore in each alternative version of your life?
    • A dashboard where you can gauge
      • Resources (Do you have the objective resources—time, money, skill, contacts—you need to pull off your plan?)
      • Likability (Are you hot or cold or warm about your plan?)
      • Confidence (Are you feeling full of confidence, or pretty uncertain about pulling this off?)
      • Coherence (Does the plan make sense within itself? And is it consistent with you, your Workview, and your Lifeview?)
    • Possible considerations
      • Geography—where will you live?
      • What experience/learning will you gain?
      • What are the impacts/results of choosing this alternative?
      • What will life look like? 
      • What particular role, industry, or company do you see yourself in?
  • Good Time Journal (Weeks 1-3)
name: Session 7: Your Brand (Mind Mapping & Odyssey Journeys)

description:
To Read:  

  • Read DYL CH 6: Prototyping
  • Read DYL CH 8: Designing Your Dream Job

To Do:

  • Create list of 5 people you could interview for each of your Odyssey Stories (15 people total)

To Submit:

  • List of 15 names of potential interviewees
name: Session 8: "Trying It On" - Prototyping

description:
To Read:  

  • Read: Good Risk Bad Risk

To Do:

  • Conduct 3 Prototyping Interviews

To Submit:

  • Write ups for three Prototyping Interviews 
    • Include (required):
      • Name, title
      • Career history
      • Description of current job
      • Description of daily activities
    • Include (ideas):
      • What is the definition of success in your current job?  What gets measured?
      • Description of a “good day” on the job
      • Description of a “bad day” on the job
      • Who succeeds in this career?  Who fails?
      • What characteristics / skills / competencies are required?
      • With what type of people do you work?
      • What prepared you well for this job?
      • Where does your career go from here?
      • What excites you about going to work each day?
      • What do you dislike the most about your job?
      • For whom would this be a great career option?
      • For whom would this be a terrible career option?
name: Session 9: Critical Pathing

description:
To Read:

  • Read: DYL Ch 10: Failure Immunity

To Do:

  • Prototyping Interviews (submit 3 total, including the one from last week)

 

To Submit:

  • Prototyping Interview Writeups
name: Session 10: Failure Immunity

description:
To Read:

  • Read: DYL Ch 9 Choosing Happiness

To Do:

  • Write 2-3 page Reflection Paper
    • Start with the objectives you wrote at the beginning of class
    • What have you learned about yourself?
    • What have you learned about the possibilities for your future?
    • Where does it go from here
  • Prepare to present your career/life-design journey to the class (5-7 minutes)
    • You may use slides or no slides
    • Focus on telling the story of the version of your future that gives you energy

To Submit:

  • Reflection Paper
  • Any makeup work

description: Dave & Lisa Discuss Life Lessons and Advice

description:
To Read:

  • “How Will You Measure Your Life” (article)

  • Create 3 North Star Word Clouds.  Instructions here.

name: Session 2: North Star

description:
Overview

To help you better understand your unique talents and how to leverage them in your professional and academic life, you will be completing the CliftonStrengths Assessment (formerly StrengthsFinder) by Gallup. Unlike traditional personality tests, this assessment identifies your natural patterns of thinking, feeling, and behaving.

Instructions

1. Purchase the Assessment: Visit the Gallup Strengths Center to purchase your access code.

• Recommended Option: **CliftonStrengths 34 ($60)**. While there is a Top 5 option ($25), I strongly recommend the Full 34 report. It provides a complete breakdown of your talent profile and more in-depth reporting on how to manage your "blind spots."

2. Take the Assessment: Set aside about 45 minutes of uninterrupted time. The test is timed, so ensure you are in a quiet environment where you can focus.

3. Download Your Reports: Once finished, please download your "CliftonStrengths 34 Report" and the "Signature Themes Report."
name: Assignment: Discovering Your CliftonStrengths
